title: Dub Commentaries
wikitext:
[]: Dub Commentaries             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Thu Mar 21, 2013 11:45 pm There's probably going to be a flame war as soon as I post this, but for anyone who never got to hear the episode dub commentaries that is mentioned here at times, and for whatever reason, wanted to hear them, a chance is out there. I'm not sure on how much the mods will allow me to link though, so for now, I'll link the first part to the commentary of the first episode and see what they think. If they're okay with it, I'll link more. If not, they're free to kill this. Episode 1

[]:             Quote Post by lastcody » Fri Mar 22, 2013 3:30 am EoE Manga commentary. My ears are still bleeding.

[]: Re: Dub Commentaries             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Mar 22, 2013 6:58 am ^ I dunno, man. Listening about Amanda Winn-Lee's first kiss while Death and Rebirth is playing is always a blast for me. (I'm not even kidding there.) The thing about English Dub Commentary is that, despite it's uselessness in understanding the series or the dub process, it's great fun to listen to is you just wanna waste an afternoon laughing at the stories told. Even Amanda herself said toward the beginning of the commentary that they weren't really gonna discuss the show, but rather just have fun talking about the stuff around it. If the listener came for something else, that should be his first and only cue to shut it off. But if you hear that and nod your head thinking, "Yeah, I'm up for that," then it's a good way to recover from Evangelion. I mean, when I want to recover it's either watching that commentary or listening to the Evaneglion VOX album , neither of which have gotten good reactions from the general Western Evangelion fandom. I guess I'm just more forgiving about the sillier comments and aesthetics around the show than most others. A useful English dub commentary is actually the one for the You Can (Not) Advance , since they actually talk about the dub process and the cast getting to know the characters for the first or maybe even third time.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Mar 22, 2013 10:32 am My favorite dub cover is the EoE one (so sue me). The commentaries are the best when the people doing them respect the show, but allow themselves to comment on it with little/no respect. It's one of the most hilarious representations of the show that I've heard. That, and there are a lot of little things that the dubbers personally thought about the show that I find amusing. Where else do you get to hear about GNR fingering Maya, or Gendo searching for his car keys inside of Rei III's boob?

[]:             Quote Post by Calico_jack89 » Sun Apr 07, 2013 2:54 pm I really love the dub commentaries. They kinda give you a good insight into how the voice actors and producers of the show understood Eva , and how they coped with such dark subject matter at times (usually with humour). Plus great little facts about production ( the filter to make the berserk Eva roar is called "grumpy flutter" or that "making out" sound effects were produced with real "making out"ha!) I pretty much purchased the platinum collection solely based on my love of the commentary.

[]:             Quote Post by Paranoid » Sun Apr 07, 2013 3:05 pm I've never listened to the commentary because I greatly dislike the dub, and I don't particularly care about what the dub VAs think of the show. That said, though, this thread kind of makes me want to listen to it just for the humorous bits. It sounds like it could be similar to watching an episode of MST3K, which I do generally like.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaminoyouni » Sun Apr 07, 2013 9:28 pm I only ever listened to the EoE commentary, before I knew any better (That is before I came to Evageeks, or EMF as it was better known prior to the shut down of the original forum) and then (last year) I met Taliesin Jaffe at Detour, who mention that because the project had left most of crew depressed afterward they were in fact drinking during the commentary which actually explains quite a bit. and although its technically not a commentary, there is.. : http://wiki.evageeks.org/Geektionary#Platinum_Special_Features

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Apr 08, 2013 6:32 pm Spike, Allison, and Amanda have great senses of humor. Just throwing that out there.
